summary: E2E testing of strict auth + verification flow + RBAC for Punto Cero Legal completed successfully. 17/17 backend tests passed (pytest) covering registration -> pending state, /auth/me reflection, admin approve/reject, RBAC (lawyer 403, socio 403 on /admin/dashboard/general). Frontend Playwright verified: lawyer register -> /verificacion-pendiente; 'Verificar estado' (data-testid=verification-check-status) stays on pending while not approved; pending lawyer hitting /dashboard or /admin via URL gets redirected to /verificacion-pendiente; admin_general (darwin) logs into /admin and sees 'Auditoría de Acceso · Compliance' section; clicking approve-{id} removes the user from the list (3 -> 2); admin trying /dashboard redirects back to /admin; socio (alejandro) logs into /admin with comercial pipeline view; approved lawyer logs in -> /dashboard and is redirected back to /dashboard when trying /admin. Landing /#planes shows payment-methods-section with MercadoPago + PayPal text; footer includes 'Inversiones y Variedades DJGG 2013' and TikTok mentions @Puntoceroconsultores + @PuntoceromultiserviciosLATAM.

minor.issue: Uses status=='active' (lowercase) to label leads as 'registered'; new approved users get status='ACTIVE' (uppercase) so they show as 'pending' in the comercial leads list. Cosmetic only, does not block flows.

action_items: (Optional) Align /api/admin/dashboard/comercial leads.status mapping to also treat 'ACTIVE' as registered (currently checks lowercase 'active').
(Optional) Consider clearing localStorage on logout button to simplify session switching in tests; functional flows already work via redirect-on-role.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py login: is_verified normalization is correct (admin roles -> True; lawyer/client -> persisted value). Good.
admin.py: approve/reject explicitly check role in ['admin','admin_general'] inside the endpoint - prevents socio_comercial bypass even though they share get_current_admin. Correctly returns 403.
server.py startup seed: backfill on each boot for master accounts ensures is_verified=True/status=ACTIVE even if DB was created earlier with legacy values.
Pydantic UserBase.status Literal allows both legacy ('active') and new ('ACTIVE','PENDING_VERIFICATION') - causes the comercial dashboard mismatch noted above. Consider a single status convention.
ProtectedRoute RBAC works for all 4 redirect cases verified via direct URL navigation (pending->/verificacion-pendiente, admin->/admin, lawyer->/dashboard).
test_credentials: Admin: darwin@puntocerolegal.com / Admin2025! | Socio: alejandro@puntocerolegal.com / Socio2025! | Lawyers: created dynamically per test (TEST_lawyer_*, test_fe_*, test_approved_*)
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_lawyer_* and test_fe_* / test_approved_* lawyer accounts were created during the run. A couple were left in PENDING_VERIFICATION (1 from FE register flow, 1 from one of the backend test pending users that wasn't approved); one (Dr Approved) was approved via API. These are clearly prefixed for future cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_auth_rbac.py is the regression file - re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_auth_rbac.py -v. It depends on the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and the two master accounts being seeded. The approve flow creates and approves a unique lawyer per run so it is idempotent. Frontend register requires phone field (data-testid='register-phone') - missing it silently fails the redirect to /verificacion-pendiente.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration. All 21 features in the request behaved as specified.
